raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Currently, California law does not consider threats against a victim’s pet as part of the intimidation pattern required to convict someone of stalking.",
    "Senate Bill 221 aims to change California law to clarify that threatening to harm a person’s pet is a form of stalking.",
    "Stalkers often use pets as a way to terrorize their victims.",
    "One in three women and one in six men in the U.S. have experienced stalking in their lifetime.",
    "Perpetrators frequently target victims’ pets as a means of instilling fear and emotional distress.",
    "A National Crime Victimization Survey found that four in ten stalkers threaten not only their victims but also their victims’ family members, friends, co-workers, or pets.",
    "Over 87,000 cases of threats against pets have been reported."
  ]
}
```
